Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.model.player

Class HuntShootingStrategy

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.model.player.HuntShootingStrategy
All Implemented Interfaces:
ShootingStrategy, Serializable
public class HuntShootingStrategy extends Object implements ShootingStrategy
A smarter alternative to RandomShootingStrategy: while there is no damaged-but-not-yet-sunk ship on the board, it behaves exactly like plain random targeting. As soon as a shot lands a CellState.HIT (a hit that hasn't sunk the ship yet), it switches to "hunting":
  • With a single, isolated hit, the ship's orientation isn't known yet, so all four orthogonal neighbors (up/down/left/right) are candidates -- same as a human player probing around a first hit.
  • Once two or more hits turn out to be orthogonally connected (almost certainly the same ship, since every ship is a straight line), the orientation IS known -- only the two cells extending that same line are tried first. If both ends turn out to be unusable (the edge of the board, or already shot), it falls back to probing every orthogonal neighbor of every hit cell in the cluster, the same as the single-hit case.

Deliberately stateless: instead of remembering what it hit last turn, it re-scans opponentBoard on every call for cells still in the HIT state, and flood-fills each cluster of connected hits to find its shape. The board itself is always the single source of truth for "what's still being hunted right now."

See Also:

    private void
    addIfInBoundsAndShootable(Board board, int row, int column, List<Position> candidates)
    Adds (row, column) to candidates if it's within the board and hasn't been shot at yet; otherwise does nothing.

  • Constructor Details

    • HuntShootingStrategy

      public HuntShootingStrategy()
      Uses a new, non-seeded Random.

    • HuntShootingStrategy

      public HuntShootingStrategy(Random random)
      Parameters:
      random - the random source to draw from (shared with the fallback random strategy, so a seeded Random makes this whole strategy's behavior reproducible for testing)

  • Method Details

    • chooseTarget

      public Position chooseTarget(Board opponentBoard)
      Chooses the next cell to shoot at on the opponent's board.
      Specified by:
      chooseTarget in interface ShootingStrategy
      Parameters:
      opponentBoard - the board being fired upon
      Returns:
      a position that has not been shot at yet

    • findHuntCandidates

      private List<Position> findHuntCandidates(Board board)
      Finds every connected cluster of hit cells and collects each one's own hunting candidates.

    • isHit

      private boolean isHit(Board board, Position position)
      Returns:
      true if position has been hit but hasn't sunk its ship yet

    • collectHitCluster

      private List<Position> collectHitCluster(Board board, Position start, Set<Position> visited)
      Flood-fills every orthogonally-connected hit cell starting from start. Since every ship is a straight line, a cluster found this way is (in practice) always exactly one damaged ship.

    • candidatesForCluster

      private List<Position> candidatesForCluster(Board board, List<Position> cluster)
      A lone hit means the orientation isn't known yet -- probe all four sides. Two or more connected hits mean the orientation IS known, so the two cells extending that established line are tried first -- but if BOTH ends are unusable (off the board, or already shot), that line is exhausted without having sunk the ship, so fall back to probing every orthogonal neighbor of every hit cell in the cluster, the same as the single-hit case.

    • allOrthogonalNeighborsOf

      private List<Position> allOrthogonalNeighborsOf(Board board, List<Position> cluster)
      Every unshot orthogonal neighbor of every cell in the cluster, combined -- the fallback once both line-ends are exhausted.

    • unshotOrthogonalNeighbors

      private List<Position> unshotOrthogonalNeighbors(Board board, Position hit)
      Returns:
      every orthogonal neighbor of hit that is still within the board and hasn't been shot at yet

    • lineExtensionCandidates

      private List<Position> lineExtensionCandidates(Board board, List<Position> cluster)
      The two cells just past each end of an already-established line of hits.

    • orthogonalNeighborsInBounds

      private List<Position> orthogonalNeighborsInBounds(Position center)
      Returns:
      the (up to four) orthogonal neighbors of center that fall within the board's 0-9 range

    • addIfInBoundsAndShootable

      private void addIfInBoundsAndShootable(Board board, int row, int column, List<Position> candidates)
      Adds (row, column) to candidates if it's within the board and hasn't been shot at yet; otherwise does nothing.
